evals.prompt: Generate a test case for any ACM JIRA ticket with a linked PR.
evals.expected_output: Pipeline completes all phases in order, MCP tools called before writing labels, quality review runs, report.py produces output files.
assertions: Phases execute in order: JIRA investigation → code analysis → UI discovery → synthesis → writing → quality review
acm-ui-source MCP is called at least once before any UI label appears in the test case
Quality review gate runs and produces review-results.json
report.py produces both test-case.md and SUMMARY.txt
No UI label, route, or selector is assumed without MCP or investigation evidence

evals.prompt: Generate a test case for a JIRA ticket in any ACM console area.
evals.expected_output: Output test case passes structural validation regardless of which console area the ticket belongs to.
assertions: Title follows pattern: # RHACM4K-XXXXX - [Tag-Version] Area - Test Name
All Polarion metadata fields present: Type, Level, Component, Subcomponent, Test Type, Pos/Neg, Importance, Automation, Tags, Release
Setup section has numbered bash commands with # Expected: comments
Test Steps section has ## Test Steps header before first ### Step N:
Steps are separated by --- dividers
Teardown section cleans up all resources created in setup with --ignore-not-found

evals.prompt: Generate test cases for tickets across different ACM areas (governance, RBAC, fleet-virt, clusters, search).
evals.expected_output: Skill produces valid output for each area. Area detection works generically, not hardcoded to specific tickets.
assertions: Area is detected from JIRA ticket content, not from a hardcoded ticket-to-area mapping
Area-specific knowledge file is read from acm-knowledge-base for the detected area
Test case conventions adapt to the area (correct tag pattern, component names, subcomponents)
No area produces an error or empty output when given a valid ticket from that area

evals.prompt: Generate a test case for a JIRA ticket with incomplete inputs (no linked PR, no ACs, or multi-story PR).
evals.expected_output: Skill handles missing inputs gracefully without crashing or producing invalid output.
assertions: No linked PR: proceeds with JIRA-only investigation or asks user for PR URL
No ACs in JIRA: derives test scenarios from description and comments instead of failing
Multi-story PR: scopes test case to the target story only, mentions others in Notes
No cluster URL for live validation: skips live validation phase and notes it explicitly

evals.prompt: Generate a test case when one or more MCP servers are unavailable.
evals.expected_output: Skill notes unavailable MCPs explicitly and proceeds with available data. Never silently assumes labels, routes, or selectors.
assertions: If acm-ui MCP unavailable: marks all UI elements as [NEEDS VERIFICATION] instead of assuming
If jira MCP unavailable: asks user for ticket details or falls back to manual input
If polarion MCP unavailable: skips Polarion duplicate check and notes it
Unavailable MCPs are reported in the output, not silently skipped
